notes: Every unlock now requires your biometric or PIN, so a stolen security key alone can't decrypt your files.
Your private key is now non-extractable: the browser will never hand it out, even to malicious code on the page.
Sensitive key material is wiped from memory right after it's used.
The offline recovery tool is locked down with a strict Content-Security-Policy, so no injected code can run. It physically can't phone home.
Disguised-filename protection strips hidden right-to-left characters from decrypted files, so a sender can't make a .exe look like a .jpg.
Safer offline behavior: only complete, successful responses are cached, so a failed load can't corrupt your offline copy.
Your saved contacts are backed up if they ever fail to load, instead of being silently cleared.
Large files now encrypt and decrypt offline on the very first run, not just after you've been online once.

A cleaner, more consistent interface: icons on every action, clearer hover cues, a redesigned first run, a refined logo, a theme that follows your system by default, and clearer messaging when saving large files.
Support for large files: encrypt and decrypt files of any size without freezing the page, with live progress and a cancel option.
Support for folders: drop a whole folder or several files and FileKey bundles them into one encrypted file.
Dark mode, with a Light / Dark / Auto setting (Auto follows your system).
Offline backup recovery codes: a 24-word recovery phrase plus a standalone offline tool, so you can still decrypt your files even if FileKey ever disappears.
A contact list for easy sharing: save people's share keys under nicknames, kept encrypted on your device, with import and export.
Receive a file securely from someone who doesn't use FileKey: share a personal link so anyone can encrypt a file to you, and only your passkey can open it.
